page.title: CORS
page.lead: CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) is a browser policy that controls which origins can consume your API. It is key to enabling legitimate integrations without exposing the server to unauthorized requests from any domain.

importance.title: Why is it important?
importance.description: Without correct CORS configuration, a frontend application can be blocked by the browser or, worse, your API can accept requests from untrusted origins.
importance.warn: Security note: CORS is disabled by default until you explicitly define the <mark class="docs-highlight">origin</mark> property.

middleware.title: Configure CORS middleware
middleware.description: To enable CORS, use the built-in <mark class="docs-highlight">cors()</mark> middleware and define allowed origins and methods.

bestPractices.title: Best practices
items: Avoid <mark class="docs-highlight">origin: "*"</mark> in APIs with credentials or sensitive data.
Define only required HTTP methods in <mark class="docs-highlight">methods</mark>.
Limit <mark class="docs-highlight">allowedHeaders</mark> to what you actually use.
If you enable <mark class="docs-highlight">credentials: true</mark>, make sure to use an explicit list of trusted origins.
